title: Phase separation enhances probability of receptor signalling and drug targeting

primary_findings.key_insight: High-fidelity signaling is inherently low probability occurrence
primary_findings.solution_mechanism: Intracellular LLPS at plasma membrane weights signaling in favor of desired outcome
primary_findings.implication: Phase separation provides mechanism for reliable signal transduction despite noisy background

signaling_probability_analysis.baseline_challenge: Random molecular diffusion creates low probability of productive signaling
signaling_probability_analysis.receptor_requirement: Sufficient numbers of engaged receptors and effectors needed
signaling_probability_analysis.outcome: Phase separation enables tuning of signaling probability

phase_separation_mechanism.fidelity_improvement: Increases probability of productive signaling pathway
phase_separation_mechanism.selectivity_mechanism: Ensures mutual exclusivity in signal initiation

llps_characteristics.consequence: Influences efficiency and specificity of receptor clustering

drug_targeting_implications.advantage: Potentially improved specificity through compartmentalized signaling

signal_transduction_framework.integrated_view: LLPS as fundamental signaling regulatory mechanism
signal_transduction_framework.evolution_perspective: LLPS likely evolved for signaling fidelity
